title: Minnesota Sues Feds to Force Release of ICE Shooting Evidence
excerpt: Minnesota's attorney general and Hennepin County attorney filed a federal lawsuit accusing the Department of Justice and DHS of categorically withholding evidence in three ICE shooting incidents, calling the obstruction 'unprecedented and alarming.'
content:
On March 24, 2026, Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison and Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty filed suit against the Departments of Justice and Homeland Security, seeking a court order compelling the federal government to turn over evidence related to three shootings by federal immigration officers during Operation Metro Surge.

  • The lawsuit covered the fatal shootings of U.S. citizens Renee Good (Jan. 7) and Alex Pretti (Jan. 24), as well as the non-fatal shooting of Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis (Jan. 14).
  • Moriarty stated that the federal government had "adopted a policy of categorically withholding evidence," including refusing Touhy requests and barring state investigators from accessing shooting scenes.
  • Federal prosecutors had already dropped assault charges against Sosa-Celis and his companion after determining that federal agents' sworn accounts were "materially inconsistent with the allegations."
  • Hennepin County's office had received over 1,000 public tips via an evidence portal, and Moriarty stated her office was investigating multiple incidents of potentially unlawful officer conduct beyond the three shootings.
"Minnesotans are seeing their federal government hide evidence and obstruct investigations into these incidents. We will not sit by and let that happen." — Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty
mediaPlaceholder: Court filing and press conference footage showing Minnesota AG Ellison and Hennepin County Attorney Moriarty
imageAlt: Minnesota Sues Feds to Force Release of ICE Shooting Evidence
